purpose.summary: This COBOL program contains error handling and termination routines for a CICS application. It logs errors to a transient data queue and terminates the CICS task.

outputs.name: CSSL
outputs.description: Error log records written to the CICS transient data queue CSSL.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph logs error information to the CICS transient data queue 'CSSL'. It retrieves the current date and time using CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands and moves the transaction ID (WS-CICS-TRANID) and program name (WS-PGM-AUTH) into the error log record. It then writes the error log record (ERROR-LOG-RECORD) to the 'CSSL' queue. If the error is critical (ERR-CRITICAL), it performs the 9990-END-ROUTINE paragraph to terminate the CICS task. The paragraph consumes WS-ABS-TIME, WS-CICS-TRANID, WS-PGM-AUTH, WS-CUR-DATE-X6, WS-CUR-TIME-X6, and ERR-CRITICAL. It produces an error log record in the CSSL queue.

This paragraph serves as an exit point for the 9500-LOG-ERROR paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific logic or data manipulation. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.

This paragraph performs the necessary steps to terminate the CICS task. It first performs the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph, presumably for cleanup or other termination procedures. Then, it executes a CICS RETURN command to terminate the task. This paragraph ensures a controlled exit from the CICS application. It calls 9000-TERMINATE and issues a CICS RETURN command. It does not consume any specific inputs, but relies on the PERFORMED 9000-TERMINATE paragraph to handle any necessary data or file operations before termination.

This paragraph serves as an exit point for the 9990-END-ROUTINE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific logic or data manipulation. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.

cics_operations.purpose: Formats the system time into YYMMDD and TIME formats.
Writes an error log record to the CICS transient data queue CSSL.
